features: License Plate Recognition, built-in database stores up to 10,000 plates for onboard search/match (or use with a compatible Speco recorder)
captures plates at vehicle speeds up to 75mph in optimal conditions
recommended capture distance 22-98ft (6.7-30m); white LED extends visibility to 330ft (100m) in low light
supports up to 60fps in high-frame-rate mode (30fps standard)
ONVIF Profile G, S, and T conformant
default web UI login is admin/1234 with a separate ONVIF password that must be matched or set independently
release_notes: Like LTS's LPR camera, this is a purpose-built plate-capture camera rather than a general security camera — the narrow, long motorized lens (8-32mm) trades wide-area coverage for plate legibility at a specific recommended distance range. White-light LPR camera: illumination is a white LED (up to ~100m), not IR (official name is '4MP White Light License Plate Recognition').

frigate.notes: ONVIF Profile G/S/T conformance confirmed. The official user manual confirms the camera exposes a distinct ONVIF configuration section with its own separate password (must be matched to the web login password or set independently) — worth checking during setup since a mismatched ONVIF password is a common cause of failed ONVIF discovery. Exact RTSP URL path not stated in the manual excerpt reviewed. LPR-specific plate-read events/metadata, if exposed, would likely be more useful for automation than raw object detection on this camera's narrow FOV.

home_assistant.notes: ONVIF Profile G/S/T conformance confirmed by the manufacturer; note the separate ONVIF password requirement described above.

blue_iris.notes: Strong ONVIF conformance makes this a likely good fit; not independently confirmed with Blue Iris specifically.
